method.labour_basis: ONS ASHE 2025 provisional SOC20 1137 annual pay GBP90,081 converted over 232 working days and 464 project days.
method.overhead_basis: 20 percent of labour cost where route uses Innovate UK/UK public-cost discipline or as a conservative public-data overhead comparator.
method.unsupported_cost_policy: Do not include materials, capital, subcontracting, travel or other direct costs until quotes, invoices, partner budgets or route-specific evidence exists.
method.currency_policy: Do not convert GBP to EUR without partner/funder-confirmed budget currency and an action-date exchange-rate source.

routes.route: Shared Island Cluster-to-Cluster Project Development Fund
routes.official_readback: Funding bands: Small EUR30k-EUR74k; Medium EUR75k-EUR149k; Large EUR150k-EUR250k. Applications require at least one cluster from Ireland and one from Northern Ireland. GBER Article 27 can cap aid at 50% of total eligible costs.
routes.recommended_cost_position: Use public-data Aureon work package as in-kind/partner work-package evidence only after an eligible cluster lead/partner is confirmed. Do not request standalone funding as R&A/Aureon.
routes.cost_option: If Article 27 applies, a EUR120,000 work package can support a EUR60,000 request inside the Small band; a EUR300,000 work package can support a EUR150,000 Large-band request. Exact currency conversion and split must be partner-confirmed.
routes.gate: Eligible cross-border cluster pair and application form required; clarification email package already staged.

routes.official_readback: UKRI future opportunities list says the route is expected in August 2026, subject to approvals, with a total fund of GBP10m and maximum award of GBP500k.
routes.recommended_cost_position: Pre-application budget basis: GBP216,194 public-data package fits within the announced GBP500k maximum award.
routes.cost_option: Stage GBP216,194 as the evidence-backed minimum Aureon work package; do not scale to GBP500k without published eligibility and additional evidence.
routes.gate: Opportunity not yet open and eligibility route not final.

routes.official_readback: Maximum funding per project EUR650k, up to 50% of total project costs; SME leads receive EUR400k-EUR500k within the grant.
routes.recommended_cost_position: Do not create a healthcare grant budget until a clinically validated health partner and eligible consortium exist. Use the public-data work package as an evidence-automation partner-cost placeholder only.
routes.cost_option: A 50% aid-intensity project would require total eligible costs of at least 2x requested grant. Use partner-confirmed Euro budget only; no standalone Aureon healthcare claim is evidenced.
routes.gate: Clinically validated solution and multi-country health consortium required.

routes.official_readback: IUK Business Connect states project costs start from EUR0.5m, up to EUR70m for largest projects, and many projects can cover up to 100% of costs; ECS resilience calls expected deadline 16 September 2026, 17:00 Brussels time.
routes.recommended_cost_position: Use Aureon as a consortium partner work package for evidence automation, impact monitoring or supply-chain assurance. The public-data UK work package is below the minimum project scale, so it must sit inside a larger consortium budget.
routes.cost_option: Stage GBP216,194 equivalent as Aureon partner work package pending consortium Euro-budget conversion; total consortium project should meet EUR0.5m+ scale.
routes.gate: Horizon consortium/topic role and Euro budget required.

routes.official_readback: Eurostars call opens 9 July 2026 and closes 10 September 2026 at 14:00 CET; UK participants must conduct funded costs in the UK, use PAYE labour, overheads are flatlined at 20% of labour costs, and UK micro entities must have at least three FTE by the deadline.
routes.recommended_cost_position: Do not stage a UK-funded Eurostars application for R&A/Aureon while the current evidence records 1 FTE; the UK micro-entity three-FTE gate is not met.
routes.cost_option: Public-data UK work package aligns with PAYE labour plus 20% overheads, but it remains a partner-search budget only until FTE and consortium requirements are met.
routes.gate: International consortium, innovative SME lead and UK three-FTE micro-entity requirement.

routes.official_readback: Current Aureon discovery records up to GBP56,000/EUR67,900 support for academic expertise and project manager salary contribution.
routes.recommended_cost_position: Use the GBP56,000 cap as the application-facing support value until the provider account is verified and academic partner/project manager structure is confirmed.
routes.cost_option: Cap request at GBP56,000; do not import the full GBP216,194 R&D work package into this route because Innovation Boost support is specifically capped and structured around academic expertise/project management support.
routes.gate: Provider account/email verification and Innovation Boost application route continuation required.

routes.official_readback: Current Aureon application is already submitted for 500,000 GPU hours; preserve submitted provider record.
routes.recommended_cost_position: No new financial request; monitor submitted compute allocation.
routes.cost_option: Submitted request remains 500,000 GPU hours, not GBP.

controls: Route cost values are staging aids and must be confirmed against partner/funder application forms before transmission.
